title: (insert into ..select) statement Bug
poc:
with (insert into) command and second with (insert into ..select) command. Both commands work well and
  I could see two rows added in result.;
create sequence "ff" as integer start with 1;

CREATE TABLE t1(timeid INT DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR "ff" PRIMARY KEY,
  a INT,
  b INT,
  c INT,
  d INT,
  e INT,
  f VARCHAR(20),
  g VARCHAR(20),
  h INT,
  i VARCHAR(10),
  j VARCHAR(10),
  k VARCHAR(10),
  l INT,
  m INT,
  n VARCHAR(10),
  o VARCHAR(10),
  p VARCHAR(10),
  q VARCHAR(15),
  r INT
  );
insert into t1(a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, i, j, k, l, m, n, o, p, q, r);

insert into t1(a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h) select  a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h
  from t1 where timeid = 1;
key_bug_features: (insert into ..select) statement fails to execute correctly
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), age INT CHECK (age >= 0), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO t2(id, name, age) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30);
INSERT INTO t2(id, name, age) VALUES (2, 'Bob', NULL);
INSERT INTO t2(id, name, age) SELECT id, name, age FROM t2 WHERE age IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE age IS NULL;
